rationale: Keep this pair because the citing paper’s core mechanism and boundary condition are disfluency-based ("perceptual disfluency" and "source attribution of difficulty"), which closely matches the cited paper’s central constructs ("preference fluency" and attribution-dependent effects on choice). The citing note explicitly says it "extends disfluency research," making the 2007 JMR article a plausible theory/mechanism source rather than a generic mention.
